eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Morgan Stanley a 50.5% implied probability as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated 2026 IPO, code-named Project Apex, reflecting its prominent role in the 21-bank underwriting syndicate confirmed in late March reports from Reuters and CNBC. Morgan Stanley banker Michael Grimes, a longtime SpaceX advisor who led the final bake-off presentation at Starbase, has positioned the firm ahead of rivals like Bank of America (15.5%) and Goldman Sachs (12.5%), amid ongoing jockeying for top bookrunner status on the potential $75 billion raise at a $1.75 trillion valuation. Recent virtual banker meetings outlined a June roadshow and late-May prospectus release, with no public S-1 yet, leaving room for shifts before resolution.

markets.question: Will Mohamed Salah play in Scottish Premiership next?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the league of next club that Mohamed Salah officially joins by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET.

If Mohamed Salah does not officially join a new club by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Mohamed Salah signs a contract that includes a delayed transfer or loan-back clause requiring him to remain at Liverpool for a specified period before joining another club, this market will resolve to the club he is officially contracted to join following that period.

If Mohamed Salah joins a club in a league that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Mohamed Salah is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional club by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

Transfers or assignments between a club’s senior team and reserve or affiliate teams will not be considered for this market.

An official transfer announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from Liverpool, Mohamed Salah and/or the acquiring club. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mohamed Salah's March 24 announcement to depart Liverpool as a free agent at the end of the 2025/26 Premier League season has ignited a fiercely competitive race for his signature, with trader consensus tightly clustered around Saudi Pro League (45.5% implied probability), Egyptian Premier League (39.8%), and Primeira Liga (39.5%). Renewed Saudi pursuits by Al-Ittihad and Al-Hilal, backed by massive financial incentives and prior summer links, edge it ahead, yet reports of Salah rejecting multimillion-pound offers for a potential European giants move—echoing Roma interest—bolster Primeira Liga bids amid Portugal's appeal for high-profile wingers. Egyptian homecoming sentiment, fueled by his national icon status and Al Ahly whispers, keeps that path viable, while MLS expansion sides like San Diego FC add pressure. This bunched pricing highlights traders' split on Salah prioritizing lucrative deals, legacy return, or competitive European football in his mid-30s.

markets.question: Will Gamba Ōsaka vs. Vissel Kōbe end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gamba Ōsaka vs. Vissel Kōbe
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J1 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between Gamba Ōsaka and Vissel Kōbe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vissel Kobe's position atop the J1 League table drives their slim 36% implied probability edge as visitors to third-placed Gamba Osaka, but the hosts' solid home record and balanced head-to-head history—15 wins apiece across 38 meetings—keep probabilities tightly clustered. Recent form underscores the contest's competitiveness: Gamba drew their last two league outings (1-1 vs Kyoto Sanga and V-Varen Nagasaki) after a home loss to Avispa Fukuoka, while Kobe grinded a 0-0 stalemate at Cerezo Osaka following AFC Champions League exertions. Key absences like Gamba's Shuto Fukuoka (hamstring) and Kobe's Takahiro Ogihara (knee surgery last week) offset each other, fueling trader consensus for a potential low-scoring draw at 29.5% amid both teams' fatigue from midweek cup ties.

markets.question: Will ThreadGuy appear on the UpOnly podcast by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a new episode of the UpOnly podcast featuring the specified guest is publicly released by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

“Released” means the full episode is published and available to the general public on an official UpOnly platform (e.g. YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or the show’s official feed).

Previews, clips, teasers, or promotional material do not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be the official UpOnly podcast channels and/or a consensus of credible public reporting.

markets.question: Will Houston Texans win the 2027 NFL AFC Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2027 AFC championship game.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2027 NFL AFC championship per the rules of the NFL (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2027 NFL AFC championship game is cancelled, postponed after March 31, 2027 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from NFL (https://www.nfl.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Ethereal FDV above $600M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ethereal's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ethereal (https://x.com/etherealdex) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: Pisa SC vs. US Lecce - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Serie A game between Pisa SC and US Lecce, scheduled for May 1, 2026 at 2:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In a pivotal Serie A relegation clash at Arena Garibaldi on May 1, Pisa SC (20th, 2-12-20 record, 18 points) hosts US Lecce (17th, 7-8-19, 29 points), both enduring dismal form—Pisa winless in four with 0.7 goals per game average, Lecce dropping their last four away. Injuries thin attacking options: Pisa misses Rafiu Durosinmi (muscular problems), Marius Marin (knee), and Matteo Tramoni; Lecce without Medon Berisha (torn thigh muscle until July) and nursing Robin Gosens' hamstring issue. Trader consensus on player props eyes low outputs amid sparse scoring (Lecce's 1-0 December win), spotlighting Pisa's Stefano Moreo and M'Bala Nzola for goals/assists, Lecce's Nikola Stulic and Lassana Coulibaly for limited chances, with midfield cards likely in heated battle.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Melanie Stansbury (D) commands trader consensus at 91% implied probability to win New Mexico's 1st Congressional District, reflecting the seat's solid Democratic lean—demonstrated by strong partisan voting index and Harris's 2024 margin—and her fundraising dominance over Republican nominee Ndidiamaka "Didi" Okpareke, a pharmacist who secured the unopposed GOP nod in March. With no public polling indicating competitiveness and primaries set for June 2, the race aligns with historical incumbency advantages in safe districts. Challenges would require a Stansbury scandal, massive GOP national midterm wave, or Okpareke fundraising surge, though structural barriers remain high.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket reflects a razor-thin split between 50-60B (46.5% implied probability) and 80-90B (46.0%) raise sizes for SpaceX's anticipated June IPO, following its confidential S-1 filing on April 1 and accelerated employee share vesting signaling an imminent late-May prospectus release. This contest stems from divergent views on dilution tolerance—conservative traders eye limited new share issuance to preserve Elon Musk's control amid a recent $800B tender offer valuation, while bulls bet on aggressive fundraising fueled by Starlink's subscriber surge past 5 million and record launch cadence. Macro factors like Treasury yields and equity volatility add swing risk, with 70-80B (34%) capturing moderate optimism ahead of the June 15 pricing week.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at a dominant 73% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, reflecting UEFA's 16 qualification slots filled by powerhouses like Germany, France, Spain, England, and playoff winners including Sweden and Bosnia, who ousted Italy in late March. South America's 20.5% follows strong CONMEBOL performers Argentina (defending champions atop standings with 38 points), Ecuador, and Colombia securing six direct spots, though depth lags behind Europe. Lower odds for Asia (3%), Africa (2.9%), North America (2.1%), and Oceania (0.3%) stem from debut qualifiers like Cape Verde and Uzbekistan facing steeper paths amid weaker overall fields. With qualifiers wrapped by March 31, recent Nations League form and group draw projections reinforce Europe's edge via superior head-to-head history and roster quality.

markets.question: Incheon United FC vs. Gangwon FC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming K-League game between Incheon United FC and Gangwon FC, scheduled for May 2 at 3:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Incheon United FC and Gangwon FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on kleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Incheon United FC vs. Gangwon FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for May 2 at 3:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incheon United, languishing in 12th place in K League 1 with just 4 points from 5 matches (1 win, 1 draw, 3 losses, 6-9 goal difference), host 6th-placed Gangwon FC (6 points, 6-6 goals) on May 2 at Sungui Arena Park. Incheon's recent form shows vulnerability, particularly at home with back-to-back losses to FC Seoul and Daejeon Hana Citizen, though a 1-0 away win over FC Anyang on March 22 offers slight momentum. Gangwon sits mid-table on balanced goal difference but inconsistent results, including a recent 0-1 loss to Pohang Steelers. No major injuries or suspensions reported; competitive head-to-head history (Gangwon 18 wins to Incheon's 14) and both teams' struggles for consistency shape trader consensus for a tight contest amid Incheon's tough upcoming fixtures against Gimcheon Sangmu and Ulsan HD FC.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Where will Trump and Putin meet next?
events.description:

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no in-person meeting between Presidents Trump and Putin by June 30 (83.7%), driven by the absence of any announced summit plans amid escalating tensions over Ukraine and Iran. Their April 29 phone call, lasting over 90 minutes, focused on a potential temporary Ukraine ceasefire around Russia's May 9 Victory Day and Iran's nuclear program, with Putin offering assistance on uranium enrichment, but yielded no venue agreement or timeline for face-to-face talks. Trump's invitation for Putin to the G20 summit in Miami this December—post-resolution—further signals deferred diplomacy, while Kremlin statements confirm no immediate meetings. Low odds on locations like China (4.7%) reflect speculation tied to Putin's regional visits, not confirmed bilateral schedules.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wellington be 14°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wellington Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wellington Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nz/wellington/NZWN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: MetService's latest forecast update, issued late April 30, projects Wellington's highest temperature on May 2 at 16°C under fine skies and easing northerlies, reflecting a stable high-pressure ridge over New Zealand that has brought settled autumn conditions after cooler recent days with highs around 14°C. Trader consensus, with market-implied odds favoring 17°C (46%) over 16°C (36%), anticipates modest upside from warmer northerly airflow and typical model spread in short-range guidance, consistent with May climatology where daily highs average 14–15°C but vary by 2–3°C due to coastal influences and insolation. Further refinements expected in today's midday forecast could shift probabilities as resolution nears.

markets.question: Spread: SC Freiburg (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "SC Freiburg" if SC Freiburg win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "VfL Wolfsburg".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Freiburg vs. VfL Wolfsburg - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: SC Freiburg holds a strong position in eighth place, riding a solid run of three recent wins and aiming to solidify mid-table security with home advantage at Europa-Park Stadion against relegation-threatened VfL Wolfsburg, who sit 17th amid a winless streak and just one victory in their last five matches. Wolfsburg's woes deepened yesterday with captain Maximilian Arnold ruled out due to a lingering groin injury, joining key absences like Jonas Wind (hamstring), Jenson Seelt (knee), and Kevin Paredes (muscle), thinning their squad for this survival scrap. Freiburg boasts balanced head-to-head history (14 wins to Wolfsburg's 13) and robust home form, while Wolfsburg's away struggles and injury crisis tilt trader consensus toward the hosts in this penultimate matchday clash.

events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for May 2 at 7:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Miami CF, fresh off a 2-2 draw in their Nu Stadium opener against Austin FC where Lionel Messi scored, holds a strong Eastern Conference position early in the 2026 MLS season as defending champions, building momentum from a 4-2 Florida Derby win over Orlando City SC on March 1. Orlando City, mired in a 1-5-0 slump highlighted by a humiliating 6-0 road loss to LAFC on April 4, faces defensive woes with injuries to Robin Jansson (broken foot), Wilder Cartagena (thigh), and others sidelined until early May. Inter Miami's home advantage, Messi's form, and superior recent head-to-head edge contrast Orlando's porous backline and poor away form, shaping trader consensus for the May 2 rematch.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Nikema Williams holds a commanding 97.8% implied probability among traders to win Georgia's 5th Congressional District Democratic primary on May 19, reflecting her entrenched incumbency advantage in the heavily Democratic Atlanta-area seat she succeeded John Lewis in holding. Recent analyses, including a report from last week, highlight her dominance over challengers like former Clayton County Sheriff Victor Hill—recently released from federal prison—Arnetress Beatty, and Andres Castro, with no public polling indicating viable competition and superior fundraising bolstering her position. Early voting began April 27 without disruptions, underscoring trader confidence. Late-breaking scandals, a turnout surge for Hill among local voters, or Williams withdrawal remain remote upset scenarios, given historical incumbent primary win rates exceeding 90% in safe districts.

G2 Esports, Natus Vincere, Karmine Corp, and Movistar KOI advanced to upper bracket semifinals via Round 2 sweeps or close wins, showcasing LEC frontrunners' depth after spring momentum. Lower bracket upsets saw Galions eliminate SK Gaming, Solary oust Fnatic, Shifters drop Vitality, and GIANTX dispatch Heretics on April 30. Traders eye May 14 clashes—G2 vs. NAVI, KC vs. MKOI (winners'), Shifters vs. Galions, GIANTX vs. Solary (losers')—as top two claim EWC slots alongside LEC Spring champ, with underdogs proving bracket volatility.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult Vladimir Putin by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks the listed individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Odds remain elevated on frequent targets like late-night hosts and conservative dissenters over Iran opposition, with the May 17 "one nation under God" gathering poised to host speeches that could trigger resolution for top options.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Western Conference first-round series knotted at 3-2 in favor of the Lakers ahead of Game 6 on May 3 at Toyota Center, Houston's gritty 99-93 Game 5 road win—despite Kevin Durant's ongoing left ankle sprain ruling him out—has injected momentum into the young Rockets squad after staving off elimination. Los Angeles battles without Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain) for the entire series, while Austin Reaves remains questionable with a left oblique issue, and LeBron James' cold jump shooting (poor percentages across recent games) has strained their offense. The Lakers hold slight regular-season edge (53-29 vs. 52-30) and home-court advantage overall, but Houston's home-floor energy, defensive rebounding, and depth could force a decisive Game 7.

markets.question: Will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit (LOW) $147 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for South Korea ETF (EWY) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the South Korea ETF (EWY) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit Week of April 27 2026?
events.description: What will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit Week of April 27 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: The iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) notched a 52-week high of $161.55 during the week of April 27, 2026, propelled by the KOSPI index's explosive 30% April rally to record levels above 6,700, with intraday peaks near 6,750. This momentum stems from semiconductor heavyweights Samsung Electronics (22% weighting) and SK hynix (19%), riding surging DRAM prices amid global AI infrastructure buildout and memory chip demand recovery. A weaker U.S. dollar and Fed rate cut expectations further bolstered exporter sentiment, evidenced by EWY's YTD return exceeding 58% and trading volume spiking to 16.7 million shares. Traders eye potential "sell in May" pullbacks alongside Q1 earnings from tech leaders and Bank of Korea policy signals as key near-term catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Abdul El-Sayed at 50% implied probability to win Michigan's open Democratic Senate primary on August 4, driven by his surge in recent Emerson and Glengariff polls tying him with Mallory McMorrow at 24% while leading among voters under 40 by wide margins. McMorrow holds second at 34.5% on stronger name recognition and appeal to older Democrats, amid reports of her fundraising edge eroding from January leads. Haley Stevens trails at 15.9%, hampered by third-place finishes despite House incumbency. Generational splits, progressive pushes like Medicare for All, and a fragmented field underscore the closely contested race since Sen. Gary Peters' 2025 retirement announcement.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: Dripmen vs FC Famalicão Esports (BO3) - CCT Europe Series #1 Play-In Group D
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Decider match between Dripmen and FC Famalicão Esports in the CCT Europe Series #1 Play-In Group D, initially scheduled for May 1 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Dripmen" if Dripmen win the match against FC Famalicão Esports.

This market will resolve to "FC Famalicão Esports" if FC Famalicão Esports win the match against Dripmen.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

Dripmen, adjusting to a fully revamped roster since April 17 (Frazehh at 1.12 rating, dobbo, Prime, robiin, Ariant0 from 8Sins), boasts mixed recent form including three series wins last week but a meager 16.7% win rate over three months and #231 HLTV ranking versus Famalicão's #192. No head-to-head history; mutual struggles on Dust2 (both <20% win rates) and Inferno could hinge map vetoes in this single-elim spot for main event entry.

High undecideds and upcoming debates could shift dynamics ahead of early voting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices 24°C (37%) and 23°C (34.5%) nearly evenly, mirroring tight spreads in ensemble guidance from ECMWF and GFS models averaging near 24°C for Shanghai's May 2 maximum, amid lingering effects of late-April low-pressure systems bringing below-normal temperatures and increased cloud cover to eastern China. Key differentiators include model divergences on afternoon boundary-layer mixing—stronger vertical mixing under clearer skies could push highs to 24°C, while persistent moisture or delayed sea breeze from the East China Sea might cap them at 23°C—compounded by subtle frontal timing uncertainties. This aligns with early-May climatological norms of 23–24°C highs at Shanghai's official observing stations. Updated 00Z/12Z runs from NOAA, ECMWF, and China Meteorological Administration expected overnight could sharpen these market-implied odds before resolution.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Deduction" at The Villages on May 1?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to visit The Villages on May 1, 2026, 3PM ET (https://www.dailycommercial.com/story/news/local/2026/04/28/president-trump-set-to-visit-the-villages-in-florida-on-may-1-2026/89835008007/). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during events at The Villages scheduled for May 1, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old interviews or prerecorded videos are aired where Trump is speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about Trump's visit to The Villages scheduled for May 1, 2026, 3PM ET. Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If this event is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, "-No Qualifying Event-" will resolve to "Yes" and all other brackets will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official measurements confirm the highest air temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C at the Observatory, solidifying 100% market-implied probability for "25°C or higher" as traders price in verified observational data from the standard Stevenson screen. This outcome aligns with an exceptionally warm April featuring a monthly peak of 30.2°C on April 16 amid anticyclonic conditions and southerly airstreams, though a late northeast monsoon brought cooler mornings on the final day. Long-term climatological trends show rising April means above historical normals of around 25°C, driven by anthropogenic warming. Scenarios challenging this near-certainty—such as a rare post-verification data audit revealing measurement error—are highly improbable given HKO's rigorous quality controls.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,000 (HIGH) in June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for June 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of June?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 closed at 7,209 on April 30, capping its strongest monthly gain since 2020 amid first-quarter 2026 earnings beats, with 84% of reporting firms exceeding estimates and overall growth tracking above 13%. The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate steady at 3.5%-3.75% in its April 28-29 meeting—the first under new leadership—despite an 8-4 dissent signaling inflation concerns after March CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year. Resilient labor data, including 178,000 nonfarm payrolls added in March, supports equity upside, though 10-year Treasury yields near 4.4% and subdued VIX around 17 reflect measured risk appetite. Key catalysts ahead: April CPI on May 12, May nonfarm payrolls, and June 16-17 FOMC.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% following the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, amid three hawkish dissents—the highest in recent memory—reflecting concerns over rebounding inflation, with March CPI rising 3.3% year-over-year, up sharply from February's 2.4%. Labor markets remain resilient, with unemployment steady at 4.3% and nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs in March. The March dot plot projects a median funds rate of 3.4% by end-2026, implying one modest cut, while CME FedWatch Tool shows over 95% probability of no change at the June 16-17 meeting. Traders price in gradual easing toward low-3% levels before 2027, with April CPI (due May 12) as the key near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will Meta have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

Watch for Claude 5 previews or rival model drops before resolution, as leaderboard lags could shift with fresh blind evaluations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a highest temperature of 23°C in Chongqing on May 1, with 99.5% implied probability, driven by the latest China Meteorological Administration (CMA) and ECMWF forecast models converging on this outcome amid persistent overcast skies, moderate rain, and northerly winds capping solar heating at the Chongqing Jiangbei International Airport station—the market's resolution source via Wunderground. Current observations show temperatures hovering in the low 20s°C under thick cloud cover, aligning with suppressed daytime highs typical during such frontal passages in early May, well below the monthly average of 27°C. Realistic challenges include unexpected afternoon clearing allowing brief solar insolation to push readings to 24°C or higher, though model ensembles indicate low likelihood before evening showers intensify; monitor hourly CMA updates for shifts.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Joe Wilson's bid for reelection in the solidly Republican SC-02 district, rated R+7 on the Cook PVI with Trump carrying it by 14 points in 2024, anchors trader consensus at 82% implied probability for a Republican Party victory in the November general election. The June 9 primaries loom as the next catalyst, pitting Wilson against challengers Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond in the GOP contest, while Democrats face a multi-candidate field including Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief. Workers Party candidate Dayna Alane Smith enters the general, boosting "Other" odds to 50.5%, but no recent polling or developments since March 30 candidate filing deadline have eroded the GOP edge, leaving Democratic Party prospects at 18%. Specific candidate outcomes A and B trade near even money amid primary uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service Los Angeles guidance emphasizes a persistent marine layer—low stratocumulus stratus fueled by cool coastal sea surface temperatures and trapped beneath a temperature inversion—returning strongly overnight into May 2, capping highs in the downtown Los Angeles basin at 68-70°F and driving trader consensus toward 68-69°F (41.5% implied probability) followed by 66-67°F (27.5%). This reflects recent observations of a 1,300-foot-deep layer yesterday with onshore flow preventing widespread burn-off, a classic May Gray pattern suppressing typical early-May averages near 72°F by 3-5°F through reduced insolation. Model consensus from GFS and ECMWF supports continuity, though earlier clearing or ridge amplification could nudge outcomes toward 70-71°F; watch morning NWS updates and hourly observations for shifts ahead of resolution based on official downtown measurements.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 500-519 tweets from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Elon Musk posting 180-199 times on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1 12:00 PM ET at 90%, reflecting his moderated late-April pace of 25-28 posts daily—down sharply from March's 42-per-day average amid high-profile distractions like xAI releases and Starlink expansions. Real-time trackers show 182 posts after seven days elapsed, synced early May 1, with the noon ET resolution leaving minimal window for deviation. Steady volume without 24-48 hour spikes from viral controversies or announcements underpins the heavy favoritism, though a late-breaking event or extended reply thread could spill into the 200-219 range at 8.5%. Historical patterns indicate low risk of 220+ surges.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Bills hold a slim 55% trader consensus to repeat as AFC East champions, bolstered by Josh Allen's elite quarterback play and a draft class reinforcing their trenches with high-upside linemen like OT Caleb Lomu, following their consistent divisional dominance amid New England Patriots' 2025 playoff deep run to the AFC Championship. The Pats sit at 34.5% after Drake Maye's breakout sophomore campaign propelled their surprise division lead and Super Bowl berth loss, but offseason roster turnover and a tougher projected schedule temper expectations despite EDGE and TE additions. Jets (7.5%) and Dolphins (7.1%) trail amid Aaron Rodgers' age/injury risks and Tua Tagovailoa's fragility, with both teams' drafts yielding modest upgrades in a rebuilding phase.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: LoL: Anyone's Legend vs Weibo Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win against Weibo Gaming in the named LoL match in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win against Anyone's Legend in the LoL match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend enters this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 riding a three-win streak, including a dominant 2-0 sweep over Invictus Gaming last week that vaulted them into fourth place with a 5-3 record, bolstering top-four direct playoff qualification hopes. Tarzan has been pivotal, earning MVP honors with elite jungle pathing under patch 26.7's meta favoring skirmishes. Weibo Gaming sits seventh at 3-6 despite a morale-boosting 2-0 upset of Top Esports days ago, but struggles with consistency amid the double round-robin format's Hard Fearless Draft. AL holds a strong head-to-head edge, winning their Week 1 clash 2-0, heightening stakes for both in the playoff race.

Alex Pereira's vacancy of light heavyweight for a heavyweight interim clash with Ciryl Gane adds intrigue, as his knockout power could propel him atop rankings with a win, keeping the market bunched under 50% across top outcomes.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Wexner testified under oath that he was "conned" by Epstein, denying knowledge of crimes, with no federal interviews or prosecutions following. events.title: Largest Company end of June?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the largest company in the world by market cap on June 30, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors NVIDIA as the largest company by market capitalization by end of June, with an implied 68.5% probability reflecting its current $4.85 trillion lead over Alphabet's $4.62 trillion, fueled by unrelenting demand for its AI GPUs amid data center expansions and hyperscaler buildouts. Alphabet's 29% odds have surged following its Q1 earnings beat on April 29, where Google Cloud revenue topped $20 billion—up sharply year-over-year—validating AI infrastructure investments like custom TPUs and a $190 billion 2026 capex plan, narrowing the gap via a nearly 10% stock spike. Lower probabilities for Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Tesla, and Saudi Aramco stem from their trailing valuations below $4 trillion, with no recent catalysts positioning them to overtake amid tech's AI-driven dominance; watch for upcoming AI benchmark releases or regulatory updates as potential swing factors.

markets.question: Will Lee Jung-hyun win the 2026 Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

events.title: Jeonnam–Gwangju Mayoral Election Winner
events.description:
The Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Min Hyung-bae's near-certain trader consensus at 98.2% stems from the Democratic Party of Korea's overwhelming historical dominance in the Jeonnam-Gwangju region, a Honam stronghold where People Power Party candidates have long polled under 20% in local elections. His April 14 primary victory over former Jeonnam Governor Kim Yung-rok solidified the nomination for the June 3 ballot selecting the inaugural integrated special city mayor, with the new entity launching July 1. PPP's sole nominee Lee Jung-hyun, confirmed April 22, vows a 30% vote target to challenge the monopoly but faces steep structural barriers absent from base rates. Odds could shift via late scandals, candidate health issues, or legal challenges before election day.

markets.question: PEC Zwolle vs. Heracles Almelo: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game between PEC Zwolle and Heracles Almelo, scheduled for May 3 at 8:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if PEC Zwolle and Heracles Almelo combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: PEC Zwolle vs. Heracles Almelo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for May 3 at 8:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: PEC Zwolle host relegation-threatened Heracles Almelo on May 3 at MAC³PARK Stadion in a crucial Eredivisie clash, with Zwolle holding 13th place on 33 points after 29 matches (GD -20) and Heracles rooted at 18th with just 19 points (GD -40). Both sides endured heavy defeats on April 5—Zwolle thrashed 5-0 by Go Ahead Eagles and Heracles routed 4-1 at Heerenveen—highlighting porous defenses amid Heracles' desperate survival fight. Zwolle benefit from home form and mid-table comfort, while Heracles eye revenge after their stunning 8-2 win over Zwolle in November 2025; key absences like Zwolle's Shola Shoretire (knee, out until early May) could influence lineups, with table positions and recent momentum shaping trader consensus on outcomes like win, goals, or clean sheets.

markets.question: Will Matteo Berrettini be the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner?
markets.description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner leads trader consensus at 47% implied probability as defending 2025 Wimbledon champion and world No. 1, propelled by a dominant 28-2 record in 2026 capped by his Monte Carlo Masters triumph over Carlos Alcaraz two weeks ago, showcasing peak form across surfaces ahead of grass preparations. Alcaraz sits second at 19% following his Australian Open title win over Djokovic in January, completing the career Grand Slam, but recent wrist injury forcing French Open withdrawal introduces fitness doubts for the July grass major. Djokovic's 6.7% reflects persistent shoulder issues prompting Miami, Monte Carlo, and Madrid skips, challenging the 39-year-old's storied All England Club pedigree. Zverev's rise to No. 3 bolsters his 3.5% share amid consistent depth.
